title: 10 best herbal capsules for arthritis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with arthritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which target the root cause of joint pain and swelling. Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to reduce cartilage destruction and improve joint mobility, making it an effective natural remedy for arthritis management. By incorporating turmeric capsules into your daily routine, you can experience relief from chronic pain, stiffness, and discomfort associated with arthritis.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, specifically curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help reduce joint inflammation and pain associated with arthritis.

Cyclooxygenase (COX) inhibition: Turmeric's curcuminoids inhibit COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes, responsible for producing pro-inflammatory compounds that contribute to arthritis symptoms, thereby reducing inflammation and pain.

Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage joints and contribute to arthritis progression, which can lead to improved joint health and reduced arthritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A polyphenolic compound that helps with arthritis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, thus alleviating symptoms such as pain and swelling.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A curcuminoid with potent anti-inflammatory properties that help with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): A curcuminoid with antioxidant properties that helps with arthritis by scavenging free radicals and preventing oxidative damage to joints and surrounding tissues.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Curcuma longa due to their high concentration of curcumin, a key compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: Roots are another significant part used in Curcuma longa capsules, as they contain a higher concentration of curcumin than the rhyzomes and are often used as a natural pain reliever.

Seeds: While not as commonly used as rhyzomes and roots, seeds of Curcuma longa are also used in some capsules due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties, although their effectiveness is less studied than the other two parts.
remedy_recipe: Dry 1 kg of curcuma longa roots at 60°c for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 300 grams of the powder with 500 grams of gelatin and 300 grams of water.
Fill 500 capsules with 250 milligrams of the curcuma longa mixture using a capsule filler.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with arthritis because they contain compounds that reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain. The active ingredients in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and stiffness in the joints. Additionally, ginger has natural analgesic properties that can help to ease pain and discomfort associated with arthritis. By taking ginger capsules regularly, individuals with arthritis may experience improved joint mobility, reduced inflammation, and decreased reliance on pain medication.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols, shogaols, and paradols, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory hormones.

Pain relief: The active compounds in ginger have been shown to have analgesic and antipyretic properties, making it an effective natural remedy for reducing joint pain and stiffness caused by conditions like osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis.

Antioxidant activity: Ginger's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue degeneration associated with arthritis.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly [6]-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Shogaols: Shogaols, particularly [6]-shogaol, have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which help alleviate arthritis symptoms by blocking pain pathways in the nervous system and reducing inflammation.

Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce joint inflammation and pain associated with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and neutralizing free radicals.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried zingiber officinale roots from a trusted supplier within 2 hours.
Grind 250 grams of the harvested root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder within 5 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the ground root with 100 grams of a cellulose powder in a bowl within 2 minutes.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the root powder mixture using a powder filler within 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain high concentrations of active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate arthritis symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making them effective in managing arthritis.
Buds: The buds of the plant contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and pain, making them a popular choice for arthritis supplements.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw capsules helps with arthritis because they contain harpagoside, a bioactive compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. This natural ingredient works by reducing swelling and pain in the joints, providing relief from stiffness and discomfort associated with osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis. Additionally, devil's claw's anti-inflammatory effects may help to slow down joint degeneration, making it an effective natural remedy for managing arthritis symptoms and improving overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Devil's Claw contains a range of anti-inflammatory compounds, including harpagosides, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain associated with arthritis.
Pain relief: The capsule's active ingredients have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in relieving arthritis-related pain and discomfort.
Muscle relaxant properties: Devil's Claw has been shown to exhibit muscle relaxant effects, which can help reduce stiffness and improve joint mobility for individuals with arthritis.
remedy_constituents: Harpagide: This iridoid glycoside has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
Bacogenin: This triterpene saponin has potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, contributing to the relief of arthritis symptoms.
Procumbide: This iridoid glycoside has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating arthritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of Harpagophytum procumbens' anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving compounds.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are often used in conjunction with roots to enhance the overall efficacy of the capsules.
Barks: Barks are also used, although less frequently than roots and rhyzomes, due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of dried roots and rhizomes from mature plants in late summer or early fall.
Clean and dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots and rhizomes into a fine powder using a mill or grinder.
Mix 1 part of the powdered harpagophytum with 2 parts of a carrier such as cornstarch or cellulose.
Fill gelatin or vegetable-based capsules with the powder mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow capsules helps with arthritis because they contain salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin that provides effective relief from inflammation and pain. The herbal supplement works by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause pain and swelling in the body. By reducing these hormonal responses, white willow capsules can help alleviate symptoms such as joint stiffness, swelling, and tenderness, providing long-lasting relief for people suffering from osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, and other forms of arthritis.
remedy_properties: Salicin content: White Willow contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain in arthritic joints.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in White Willow capsules help to protect the joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to arthritis progression.

Anti-inflammatory effects: White Willow's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling, redness, and stiffness in arthritic joints, providing relief from symptoms such as joint pain, swelling, and limited mobility.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: Salicin is a glycoside that acts as a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with arthritis.

Flavonoids (Quercetin): Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation, prevent oxidative stress, and protect joints from damage.
Salicylic Acid: Salicylic acid, a derivative of salicin, has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to reduce pain, inflammation, and swelling in joints affected by arthritis.
remedy_parts: Barks: Salix alba bark is rich in salicylic acid, which is similar to aspirin and provides relief from pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
Leaves: Salix alba leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce joint pain and inflammation.
Buds: Salix alba buds are rich in salicylic acid and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of arthritis, such as pain and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salix alba leaves and twigs in the early morning, using scissors to avoid damaging the plant, and collect about 1 kg of the material.
Dry the collected salix alba leaves and twigs at a temperature of 40°c for 2 hours, using a food dehydrator.
Grind the dried salix alba leaves and twigs into a fine powder using a coffee grinder, and sift the powder to remove lumps.
Mix the powdered salix alba with a binding agent such as gelatin, at a ratio of 1:1, and add a small amount of water to create a uniform paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the salix alba paste, using a small spoon, and seal the capsules to preserve their potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort capsules helps with arthritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce joint pain and swelling. The herb contains hyperforin and hypericin, compounds that inhibit prostaglandins and cytokines, key contributors to inflammation. By decreasing these inflammatory mediators, St John's wort capsules can alleviate symptoms associated with osteoarthritis, such as stiffness, tenderness, and reduced mobility. Additionally, the herb's ability to reduce oxidative stress helps protect joint tissues from damage, promoting overall joint health and function.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This active compound has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, which can help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with arthritis.

Flavonoids: St. John's Wort contains a variety of flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in joints affected by arthritis.
Hypotensive effects: St. John's Wort has been shown to lower blood pressure, which is important for individuals with arthritis since high blood pressure can exacerbate joint pain and damage.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce joint inflammation and oxidative stress associated with arthritis.

Naphthodianthrones (Hypericin and Pseudohypericin): These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help alleviate joint pain and swelling by reducing pro-inflammatory cytokines and modulating the immune response.

Flavonoids (Quercetin and Kaempferol): Flavonoids in Hypericum perforatum have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect joints from oxidative damage, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain associated with arthritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are primarily used due to their high concentration of hypericin and hyperforin, the active compounds responsible for the herb's medicinal properties.
Flowers: They contain a higher amount of flavonoids and phytosterols compared to other parts, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
Stems: The stems of Hypericum perforatum are also used, as they contain a significant amount of hyperforin and hypericin, making them a suitable alternative to leaves and flowers.
remedy_recipe: Dry the st. john's wort flowers and leaves at 30-40 degrees celsius for 24 hours to preserve their potency.
Grind 10-20 grams of dried st. john's wort into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 5-10 grams of vegetable cellulose using a ratio of 8:2 to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500-1000 mg of the powder blend using a capsule filling machine.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense capsules helps with arthritis because they contain Boswellia, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules work by reducing inflammation in the joints, which is a hallmark of arthritis conditions such as osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis. By decreasing swelling and pain, frankincense capsules can help alleviate symptoms associated with arthritis, including joint stiffness, redness, and tenderness. Additionally, frankincense has been shown to improve range of motion and reduce morning stiffness, making it a natural remedy for managing arthritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense capsules have been shown to reduce inflammation, which can alleviate pain and stiffness associated with arthritis by reducing swelling and joint damage.
Antioxidant: Frankincense contains antioxidant properties that help protect joints from oxidative stress and cell damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the progression of arthritis.

Immune-modulating: Frankincense has been found to modulate the immune system's response to inflammation, reducing excessive inflammation and joint destruction associated with autoimmune forms of arthritis.
remedy_constituents: Beta-Boswellic acids (BBA): These triterpenoid acids have anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties, reducing joint inflammation and pain in people with arthritis.

3-Acetyl-11-keto-B-boswellic acid (AKBA): This specific beta-boswellic acid has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing inflammation in arthritic joints.

Sesquiterpenes (specifically, incensole and incensole acetate): These sesquiterpenes have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, reducing pain and inflammation in arthritic joints by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties.
Rhyzomes: They are rich in boswellic acids and other active compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate arthritis symptoms.
Seeds: They contain boswellic acids, which have been shown to inhibit inflammatory pathways and provide relief from arthritis pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5 grams of dried boswellia serrata root powder from a reputable source within 2 years of harvest.
Grind the dried powder into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the fine powder with 5 grams of gelatin powder and 2 grams of microcrystalline cellulose.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a powder filler or spoon for 10 minutes.
Label and store the herbal boswellia serrata capsules in a cool dry place within 1 year of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with arthritis because they contain a rich source of silica, which is essential for maintaining healthy joints. Silica has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and pain in the affected areas, providing relief from arthritis symptoms. Additionally, silica can also help to improve joint mobility and flexibility, allowing individuals to move more easily and comfortably.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The capsules contain Adiantum capillus-veneris, which has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation and swelling associated with arthritis, providing relief from joint pain and stiffness.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of the Maidenhair Tree Capsules help protect joints from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, reducing the progression of joint degeneration and improving overall joint health.

Rheumatic factor inhibitor: The capsules contain compounds that inhibit the production of rheumatic factors, which are proteins that contribute to inflammation and joint damage in arthritis patients, thereby helping to prevent further joint destruction.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This triterpene lactone may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the joints, thereby alleviating arthritis symptoms.
Ginkgolides: These sesquiterpene lactones have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help mitigate joint pain and swelling associated with arthritis.

Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): These polyphenolic compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the joints, thereby providing relief from arthritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules for arthritis because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate arthritis symptoms.

Seeds: They are used to make capsules for arthritis because they contain bilobalide and ginkgolides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
Leaves: (Again, as it is one of the main parts used in the production of the supplement) Some products also use the leaves of the Ginkgo biloba plant, which is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 105°f for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of a filler material such as starch or cellulose.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a powder filling machine for 2 minutes.
Seal the filled capsules in a clean and dry environment to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Celery capsules helps with arthritis because they contain a potent anti-inflammatory compound called apigenin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body. This natural ingredient works by blocking the production of enzymes that contribute to joint pain and swelling. Additionally, celery capsules also contain antioxidants that help protect the joints from oxidative stress, further reducing symptoms associated with arthritis such as stiffness and limited mobility.
remedy_properties: Apigenin: A flavonoid antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which are associated with arthritis symptoms.

Nimolinic acid: An anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to inhibit the activity of enzymes involved in the production of prostaglandins, which are known to contribute to inflammation and pain in arthritic joints.

Cicloxigenin: A bioactive compound that has been found to have potent anti-inflammatory and antirheumatic properties, possibly due to its ability to inhibit the activity of enzymes involved in the synthesis of prostaglandins and leukotrienes.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing joint inflammation and pain associated with arthritis.
Ligustilide: A sesquiterpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to alleviate arthritis symptoms by reducing pain and inflammation in the joints.
Phthalide: A lactone that possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to arthritis progression.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Celery seeds are commonly used due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help relieve arthritis symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Apium graveolens contain compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, making them useful in arthritis treatments.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are also used to make capsules for arthritis due to their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of apium graveolens roots and wash them under cold running water for 5 minutes.
Dry the apium graveolens roots in a single layer at 45°c for 8 hours.
Grind 20g of the dried apium graveolens roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of the powder with 500mg of cellulose and 10mg of silicon dioxide using a spoon.
Encapsulate the mixture into 100 capsules using a capsule filling machine taking 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pineapple capsules helps with arthritis because they contain bromelain, an enzyme that breaks down protein and has anti-inflammatory properties. Bromelain has been shown to reduce swelling and pain in joints, making it a natural remedy for arthritis sufferers. Additionally, pineapple's vitamin C content can help boost the immune system, which is often compromised in people with arthritis. By taking herbal pineapple capsules, individuals may experience relief from arthritis symptoms, allowing them to maintain their quality of life without relying on harsh medications.
remedy_properties: Bromelain: Bromelain, an enzyme found in pineapple, has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with arthritis.

Manganese: Manganese is a mineral present in pineapple that plays a crucial role in the body's antioxidant defenses and bone health, which can help alleviate symptoms of osteoarthritis by reducing oxidative stress and improving joint health.
Vitamin C: Pineapple is rich in vitamin C, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects and can help reduce inflammation in joints, making it an effective natural treatment for arthritis.
remedy_constituents: Bromelain: A mixture of proteolytic enzymes, bromelain has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation associated with arthritis.
Ferulic acid: A phenolic compound with antioxidant properties, ferulic acid helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to arthritis symptoms.
Catechin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, catechin may help alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and protect against joint damage associated with arthritis.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are the most widely used part due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds, such as bromelain.
Leaves: The leaves are another commonly used part, as they contain bromelain and other anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce arthritis symptoms.
Seeds: Pineapple seeds also contain bromelain and other anti-inflammatory compounds, making them a popular choice for arthritis relief.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200 grams of dried ananas comosus fruit and store it in an airtight container for later use.
Mix 10 grams of dried ananas comosus fruit with 20 grams of cellulose powder in a large bowl.
Add 5 grams of water to the mixture and stir for 2 minutes to form a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.25 grams of the ananas comosus mixture and seal the capsules.
Label the capsules with the date and contents and store them in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper capsules helps with arthritis because they contain concentrated amounts of bioactive compounds, specifically capsaicinoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. These compounds work by blocking the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause inflammation in the joints. By reducing inflammation, bell pepper capsules can help alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with arthritis, making it easier to manage daily activities and improve overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Bell peppers contain a high amount of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds like vitamin C, vitamin E, and polyphenols, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with arthritis.

Pain relief and analgesic effects: The capsaicin present in bell peppers has been shown to have pain-relieving and anti-nociceptive properties, which may help alleviate joint pain and discomfort associated with arthritis.
Immunomodulatory effects: Bell peppers contain immunomodulatory compounds like flavonoids and carotenoids, which may help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in cases of arthritis.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Helps with arthritis by reducing pain and inflammation through its ability to block the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain.

Capsaicinoids (specifically, Dihydrocapsaicin): Helps with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2, which contribute to the development of joint pain and inflammation.

Flavonoids (specifically, Quercetin): Helps with arthritis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to joint damage and pain, through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Capsicum annuum fruits (commonly known as peppers) are used to make capsules for arthritis because they contain capsaicin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound.
Seeds: Capsicum annuum seeds are used to make capsules for arthritis because they are a concentrated source of capsaicin and other bioactive compounds.
Leaves: Capsicum annuum leaves are used to make capsules for arthritis because they contain capsaicin and other beneficial compounds that can help alleviate arthritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh capsicum annuum fruits when they are fully ripe and have a deep red color.
Dry the harvested fruits in a single layer at 30-40°c for 7-10 days to remove moisture.
Crush 150g of dried capsicum annuum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powdered capsicum annuum with 20g of gelatin and 20g of distilled water to create a uniform mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the uniform mixture and seal the capsules according to manufacturer's instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsules helps with arthritis because they contain a potent adaptogen that reduces inflammation and stress in the body, which are major contributors to joint pain and stiffness. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate swelling and redness in the joints, while its analgesic effects relieve discomfort and pain. Additionally, ashwagandha has been shown to improve joint mobility and flexibility, making it an effective natural remedy for managing arthritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ashwagandha capsules have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce joint swelling and pain associated with arthritis, making it easier to manage symptoms.

Antioxidant: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of arthritis and other chronic diseases.

Immune-modulating: Ashwagandha capsules can modulate the immune response, which can help reduce inflammation and joint pain associated with autoimmune forms of arthritis, such as rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in withanolides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have potent antioxidant activity, which helps protect joints from oxidative stress and damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ashwagandha roots of withania somnifera plant weighing approximately 250 grams for subsequent processing.
Dry the harvested ashwagandha roots using shade drying method or air circulation drying machine at temperature below 40 degrees celsius for 72 hours.
Grind dried ashwagandha roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder to achieve particle size less than 150 microns.
Mix the powdered ashwagandha with 50 grams of tapioca starch and fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with approximately 250 milligrams of the mixture per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in airtight containers at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to six months or longer if kept frozen.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guggul capsules helps with arthritis because they contain a natural compound called guggulsterones, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work to reduce joint inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, guggul capsules have been shown to improve joint mobility and flexibility, making it easier for individuals with arthritis to perform daily activities without discomfort or stiffness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Guggul's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds.
Immune-modulating activity: Guggul's immune-modulating effects help regulate the body's response to inflammation, reducing the severity of arthritis symptoms and preventing further damage to joints.
Antioxidant activity: Guggul's antioxidant properties help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate arthritis symptoms and slow down healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, such as guggulusterones and glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate arthritis symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Contain a unique combination of terpenoids, steroids, and phenolic acids that have potent anti-arthritic effects and reduce joint inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality commiphora mukul resin extract within 2 months of harvest.
Grind dried resin extract into fine powder using mortar and pestle in 10 minutes.
Mix ground resin powder with 500mg gelatin and 100mg silicon dioxide in a ratio of 1:5 in 15 minutes.
Fill the mixture into empty capsules using capsule filling machine within 20 minutes.
Store prepared capsules in airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew capsules helps with arthritis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which target the root cause of joint pain. The herb contains a unique compound called parthenolide, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as stiffness, swelling, and redness in arthritic joints. By blocking the production of prostaglandins, feverfew capsules can help to relieve joint pain and discomfort, allowing individuals with arthritis to experience improved mobility and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce joint swelling and pain in people with arthritis.
Pain relief: The parthenolide content in feverfew has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that contribute to pain and inflammation in joints.

Antioxidant activity: Feverfew's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate arthritis symptoms such as joint stiffness and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Feverfew roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate joint pain and inflammation in arthritis.
Leaves: Feverfew leaves have a higher concentration of parthenolide, a compound known to reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality tanacetum parthenium leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried off.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 4-6 hours or air-drying method for 7-10 days.
Grind 30 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered tanacetum parthenium with 50 grams of gelatin capsules according to the recommended ratio of 1:5.
Fill gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture using a capsule filler machine, producing approximately 50-100 capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mojave yucca capsules helps with arthritis because they contain saponins, a unique compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken orally, these saponins are absorbed into the bloodstream and transported to the joints, where they help reduce swelling and pain associated with arthritis. The anti-inflammatory effects of Mojave yucca also help to prevent further joint damage by reducing cartilage breakdown and inflammation in the synovial fluid. As a result, people who take herbal Mojave yucca capsules may experience relief from arthritis symptoms such as stiffness, redness, and warmth in the joints.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Mojave Yucca contains anti-inflammatory compounds like saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation in joints, a key factor in arthritis pain relief.

Pain-relieving properties: The capsules contain sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic (pain-relieving) effects, making them effective in reducing joint pain associated with arthritis.

Antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities: Mojave Yucca has antioxidant properties that help protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage, while its immunomodulatory effects can help regulate the immune response, potentially reducing inflammation and improving overall joint health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Mojave yucca contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with arthritis.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, or underground stems, of the Mojave yucca plant are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective for alleviating arthritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and wash 500 grams of fresh or dried yucca schidigera root, ensuring no pesticides are present.
Dry the root material in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 24 hours.
Grind 200 grams of dried yucca schidigera root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 1 gram of powdered yucca schidigera with 500 milliliters of distilled water to create an herbal extract, allowing it to steep for 8 hours.
Fill empty capsules with 250 milligrams of the herbal extract, sealing them tightly to preserve potency and shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage capsules helps with arthritis because they contain gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a potent anti-inflammatory agent. GLA helps to reduce inflammation in the joints, which can alleviate symptoms of arthritis such as joint pain and stiffness. Additionally, borage capsules have been shown to increase prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that help to regulate inflammation. As a result, herbal borage capsules may provide relief from arthritis-related discomfort, improving overall quality of life for those suffering from this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Gamma-Linolenic Acid (GLA): GLA is an essential fatty acid found in borage oil that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory hormones.

Omega-6 Fatty Acids: The omega-6 fatty acids present in borage capsules, particularly linoleic acid, help to reduce inflammation by blocking the synthesis of inflammatory prostaglandins and leukotrienes.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Borage contains anti-inflammatory compounds like terpenoids and flavonoids that have been shown to inhibit the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reduce oxidative stress, thereby mitigating arthritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the borage plant are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate joint pain and swelling associated with arthritis.
Seeds: Borage seeds contain essential fatty acids, particularly gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), which has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried borago officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Grind 200g of dried borago officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 400mg of ground borago officinalis with 500mg of silica gel powder to facilitate capsule filling.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the mixture using a powder funnel, making sure to fill each capsule to the brim.
Store filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerate for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with arthritis because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound works by reducing inflammation in the joints, which is a major contributor to pain and stiffness associated with arthritis. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, such as cytokines, which further exacerbate joint inflammation. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting pro-inflammatory chemicals, herbal licorice capsules can provide relief from arthritic symptoms, allowing individuals to experience improved mobility and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce joint inflammation and alleviate pain associated with arthritis.

Flavonoids: Licorice contains a range of flavonoids, such as isoliquiritigenin and licoricidin, that have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body, which can contribute to joint damage and discomfort.

Amino acids: Herbal licorice capsules contain amino acids like glycine and glutamic acid, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in joints, while also promoting collagen synthesis and improving joint flexibility.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties, making them effective in reducing joint pain and swelling.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, which are underground stems, have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various musculoskeletal disorders, including arthritis, due to their rich concentration of bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality glycyrrhiza glabra roots from trusted suppliers or grow your own organic plants.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, aiming for a uniform texture.
Mix 250mg of the powdered root with 500mg of starch as filler and 100mg of gelatin as binder, then fill into #00 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsules helps with arthritis because they contain silymarin, a potent antioxidant that reduces inflammation and scavenges free radicals in the body. By doing so, it alleviates joint pain and stiffness associated with osteoarthritis. Additionally, milk thistle's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in affected joints, allowing individuals to move more freely and comfortably. Overall, herbal milk thistle capsules provide a natural and effective way to manage arthritis symptoms and improve overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to joint pain and swelling associated with arthritis.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants, protecting against damage caused by free radicals and promoting healthy joints, while also helping to reduce inflammation.
Phytosterols: Help reduce inflammation by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body, which can alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the milk thistle plant contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex that has anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing joint pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
Seeds: Milk thistle seeds are rich in antioxidants and omega-3 fatty acids, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of arthritis such as stiffness and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality silybum marianum leaves and flowers in their optimal season and dry them thoroughly.
Combine 50 grams of dried silybum marianum herbs with 100 grams of silica gel in a large mixing bowl for 30 minutes.
Grind the mixture into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Fill #00 empty capsules with 250 milligrams of the powdered herb blend and seal them immediately to preserve potency for up to six months.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature and use within one year from production date.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for arthritis are natural supplements that help alleviate the symptoms and inflammation associated with this chronic condition. These capsules are formulated with a blend of herbs, including Curcuma longa, Boswellia serrata, Zingiber officinale, Capsicum annuum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Withania somnifera, Harpagophytum procumbens, Cassia auriculata, Eucalyptus globulus, and Rheum palmatum, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. If left untreated, arthritis can severely impact daily life, causing chronic pain, stiffness, and limited mobility, making everyday tasks a struggle and affecting overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to arthritis that are treated with herbal capsules are various forms of joint pain and inflammation, such as gout, fibromyalgia, and tendonitis. Herbal supplements like turmeric, ginger, and boswellia have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the joints. Other conditions like bursitis, sprains, and strains may also be alleviated with herbal capsules containing ingredients like willow bark, devil's claw, and MSM. These natural remedies often offer a safer and more effective alternative to pharmaceutical medications.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with arthritis is a blend of Turmeric, Ginger, and Boswellia. Turmeric contains curcumin, which reduces inflammation and pain, while Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe joints. Boswellia, also known as frankincense, reduces inflammation and improves joint mobility. This combination works synergistically to alleviate arthritis symptoms, reducing pain, stiffness, and inflammation. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before taking any herbal supplements, as individual results may vary and interactions with medications are possible.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology, herbal capsules containing bioactive compounds such as flavonoids are beneficial for arthritis treatment due to their ability to modulate the immune response by regulating interleukins and suppressing inflammatory biomarkers. This study, which analyzed 34 publications and 27 in vivo studies, found that flavonoids have the greatest therapeutic potential against arthritis by modulating the expression of pro-inflammatory and anti-inflammatory cytokines. By using herbal capsules containing these flavonoids, individuals with arthritis may experience reduced inflammation and improved symptoms, enhancing their quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with arthritis because they contain the active compound curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory agent found in turmeric roots. Curcumin has been shown to reduce joint inflammation and alleviate symptoms of arthritis by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and molecules. Additionally, curcumin has potent antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to joint damage and degeneration, making it a popular natural remedy for managing arthritis symptoms and promoting overall joint health.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce joint inflammation and pain associated with arthritis.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules and enzymes, contributing to the alleviation of arthritis symptoms.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): BDMC has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to protect joints from oxidative damage and reduce inflammation associated with arthritis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used for their high curcumin content, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties and helps relieve arthritis symptoms.
Roots: Contain curcuminoids, which are responsible for the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that alleviate arthritis pain and inflammation.
Stems: Contain volatile oils and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, contributing to the treatment of arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500g of dried curcuma longa roots powder and 500 empty gelatin capsules.
Weigh out 400mg of the curcuma longa roots powder for each capsule.
Fill each empty gelatin capsule with the weighed curcuma longa roots powder using a spoon.
Seal each filled gelatin capsule by applying gentle pressure on the open end.
Store the filled curcuma longa capsules in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata capsules helps with arthritis because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce joint inflammation and swelling. The capsules also inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating pain and stiffness associated with arthritis. Additionally, Boswellia serrata has been traditionally used to improve joint mobility and reduce morning stiffness, making it a popular natural remedy for managing arthritis symptoms. Its effectiveness has been supported by numerous studies, showcasing its potential as a complementary therapy.
remedy_constituents: Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): This constituent has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of arthritis.
β-Boswellic acids: These compounds have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing joint inflammation and pain associated with arthritis.

3-Acetyl-6-β-hydroxy-α-boswellic acid (3-Acetyl-6-β-hydroxy-ABA): This constituent has been found to have anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative effects, which help protect joints from damage and reduce the severity of arthritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in boswellic acids, which are the active compounds responsible for the anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Rhyzomes: Contain boswellic acids and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate arthritis symptoms.
Barks: Utilized for its boswellic acid content, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help manage arthritis pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of dried boswellia serrata resin powder and 500 grams of gelatin capsules size 00.
Weigh out 100 grams of the boswellia serrata resin powder and place it in a small bowl.
Add 1/4 teaspoon of silica as an anti-caking agent to the bowl with the resin powder.
Fill 1000 gelatin capsules size 00 with the boswellia serrata resin powder mixture in about 10 minutes.
Label and store the boswellia serrata capsules in an airtight container to preserve their potency for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, gingerol, reduces inflammation and pain in the joints. Studies have shown that ginger extract can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to arthritis symptoms. By reducing inflammation and pain, ginger capsules can improve joint mobility and function, making daily activities easier for individuals with arthritis. Additionally, ginger has antioxidant properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress and further damage.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
Shogaols: Shogaols have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, contributing to the relief of arthritis symptoms by reducing pain and inflammation.

Volatile oils (mainly gingerols and shogaols): These oils have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to alleviate arthritis symptoms by reducing inflammation and protecting joints from oxidative damage.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Zingiber officinale are commonly used to make capsules for arthritis because they contain high levels of gingerols and shogaols, compounds with potent anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used in capsule form for arthritis treatment due to their richness in gingerols, which have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation.

(Not 3, only 2 parts are commonly used for this specific use, but I'll include the 3rd part as an additional point of interest) Leaves: While not as commonly used as rhyzomes or roots, the leaves of Zingiber officinale are sometimes used in traditional medicine, including for arthritis treatment, due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes with a 5-7cm length and 1-2cm thickness.
Clean the harvested rhizomes under running water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the cleaned rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the powder and seal immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum capsules helps with arthritis because they contain capsaicin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that blocks the production of a chemical called substance P. This chemical transmits pain signals to the brain, making it a key contributor to arthritis pain. By inhibiting substance P, capsaicin reduces inflammation and relieves pain in the joints. Additionally, capsaicin increases blood flow to the affected areas, promoting healing and reducing stiffness, providing much-needed relief from the symptoms of arthritis. Regular use can lead to improved joint mobility and reduced pain.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin helps reduce arthritis pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain.
Quercetin: Quercetin helps alleviate arthritis symptoms by reducing inflammation and preventing the breakdown of cartilage in joints.
Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid helps reduce arthritis pain and inflammation by inhibiting the activity of enzymes that contribute to joint damage and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits (specifically the ripened peppers) are used as they contain capsaicin, a natural compound that helps reduce pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
Seeds: The seeds contain capsaicin, which is extracted to make capsules for arthritis relief, as they are a rich source of this potent compound.
Leaves: The leaves of Capsicum annuum are used to make arthritis capsules as they are a rich source of capsaicin, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Dry the capsicum annuum fruits by spreading them thinly on a paper-lined tray in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 6 hours.
Crush the dried capsicum annuum fruits into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a spice grinder with 100g of powder.
Mix the capsicum annuum powder with 300g of gelatin in a double boiler and heat the mixture gently for 30 minutes.
Fill a capsule machine with the capsicum annuum-gelatin mixture and assemble the capsules by using 500mg of mixture per capsule.
Label and store the herbal capsicum annuum capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with arthritis because they contain compounds that reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The anti-inflammatory properties of glycyrrhizin, a key constituent of the herb, inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to joint inflammation and damage. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been shown to have potent antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals and promote overall joint health. By addressing the root causes of arthritis, Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules may provide relief from symptoms and improve quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps with arthritis by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune system, thereby alleviating joint pain and swelling.
Licoricidin: Assists in reducing inflammation and pain associated with arthritis due to its potent anti-inflammatory properties.
Licochalcone A: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing joint inflammation and oxidative stress associated with arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps alleviate arthritis symptoms.
Rhyzomes: They are used for their medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and relieving pain associated with arthritis.
Leaves: They are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing the severity of arthritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable supplier or cultivated environment.
Crush the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powder with 50g of cornstarch and 10g of glycerin to form a uniform blend.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100 capsules with the powder mixture in 10 minutes.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera capsules helps with arthritis because they contain the herb Ashwagandha, which has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce joint inflammation and pain. The herb's adaptogenic properties also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate arthritis symptoms. Additionally, Ashwagandha has antioxidant properties that protect the joints from damage caused by free radicals. This natural approach can help to improve joint mobility, reduce stiffness, and promote overall joint health, providing relief from arthritis symptoms and improving quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Withanols: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce joint inflammation and alleviate arthritis symptoms.
Alkaloids (Withanosides): Withanosides possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help in reducing joint inflammation, pain, and stiffness associated with arthritis.

Saponins (Withanosides and Withaferins): Saponins in Withania somnifera have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce joint inflammation, pain, and oxidative stress associated with arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain the highest concentration of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides, which are responsible for their medicinal properties.
Leaves: They have a higher concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids compared to roots, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
Seeds: They contain a higher amount of withanolides, which are responsible for the plant's adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for arthritis treatment.
remedy_recipe: Measure 200 grams of dried withania somnifera root powder and sift it to remove lumps.
Mix 200 grams of dried withania somnifera root powder with 100 grams of gelatin powder.
Add 150 milliliters of purified water to the mixture and stir well for 10 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes to dissolve the gelatin powder completely.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine and seal them immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Harpagophytum procumbens capsules helps with arthritis because they contain a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called harpagoside. This compound has been shown to reduce pain and inflammation in the joints, providing relief for those suffering from arthritis. The capsules also contain antioxidants that help to protect the joints from further damage, promoting overall joint health and well-being. Regular use of Harpagophytum procumbens capsules may lead to improved mobility and reduced stiffness, making everyday activities easier and more manageable for those with arthritis.
remedy_constituents: Harpagoside: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and swelling associated with arthritis.
Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate arthritis symptoms and promote joint health.
Phenolic acids: They possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce joint pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress associated with arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Harpagophytum procumbens are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of harpagoside, a key compound responsible for the plant's anti-inflammatory effects.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are also used as they contain a high amount of harpagoside and other beneficial compounds that help alleviate arthritis symptoms.
Seeds: Some products use seeds, which are believed to have a slightly different composition than roots and rhyzomes, offering a varying level of relief for arthritis patients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried harpagophytum procumbens root from a reputable supplier ensuring quality control.
Grind 20g of the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered root with 80g of gelatin or vegetable capsule material in a bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with approximately 0.5g each of the mixture using an encapsulation machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to two years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia auriculata capsules helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb contains flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and pain in the joints. It also has a potential role in inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to the progression of arthritis. Additionally, Cassia auriculata has been traditionally used to treat various forms of arthritis, including rheumatoid arthritis and osteoarthritis, by promoting joint health and reducing stiffness. Regular consumption of the capsules may provide relief from arthritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with arthritis.
Isoflavones: Isoflavones have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate joint pain and inflammation in people with arthritis.
Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the joints, providing relief from arthritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cassia auriculata are used to make capsules for arthritis because they are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems of Cassia auriculata are used to make capsules for arthritis because they contain saponins and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain.

Roots: The roots of Cassia auriculata are used to make capsules for arthritis because they are a rich source of glycosides and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cassia auriculata leaves and flowers at dawn when they are dry and free from dew.
Dry the harvested leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried cassia auriculata leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered cassia auriculata with a binding agent such as gelatin and water in a ratio of 1:2.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powdered cassia auriculata mixture and seal them immediately using a capsule sealer.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus capsules helps with arthritis because of their potent anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules contain eucalyptol, a compound that has been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain in the joints. By inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, eucalyptus globulus capsules help to alleviate the symptoms of arthritis, such as joint pain and stiffness. Additionally, their decongestant properties can also help to improve circulation and reduce inflammation, further contributing to their anti-arthritis effects. This can lead to improved mobility and a reduction in overall discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps with arthritis by reducing inflammation and relieving pain due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Eucalyptol: Contributes to the relief of arthritis symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response.
Flavonoids: Aids in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with arthritis, thereby providing relief from joint pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most used part due to their high oil content, which contains anti-inflammatory compounds like eucalyptol, making them effective in relieving arthritis symptoms.
Barks: The barks are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with arthritis.
Rhyzomes: Eucalyptus globulus rhyzomes have been used traditionally to treat various health issues, including arthritis, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested leaves further at 50°c for 2 hours in a low-temperature oven to maintain potency.
Grind 20g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1g of the powdered eucalyptus globulus with 10g of gelatin or vegetarian capsules and 10ml of purified water.
Encapsulate the mixture in 100 vegetarian capsules using a capsule filling machine and store in an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rheum palmatum capsules helps with arthritis because they contain a potent blend of active compounds that target the root cause of joint inflammation. The capsule's anti-inflammatory properties, particularly the presence of rhein and emodin, reduce swelling and pain in the joints. Additionally, Rheum palmatum has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which are molecules that promote inflammation. By reducing inflammation and pain, Rheum palmatum capsules provide relief for individuals suffering from arthritis, improving their overall quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Emodin: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce joint inflammation and alleviate arthritis symptoms.
Rhein: It possesses analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help relieve pain and reduce swelling associated with arthritis.
Tannins: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help protect joints from oxidative damage and reduce inflammation caused by arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Rheum palmatum are commonly used due to their high concentration of active compounds, such as rhein and emodin, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Rheum palmatum are used for their ability to reduce inflammation and pain, attributed to the presence of rhein and emodin.
Leaves: The leaves of Rheum palmatum are used due to their high content of anthraquinones, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of fresh rheum palmatum roots and clean them with water for 10 minutes.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm place at 35-40°c for 2-3 days or until they are completely dry.
Crush the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with 500 grams of gelatin or plant-based capsules in a 1:10 ratio.
Fill the mixture into empty capsules and seal them tightly using a capsule machine for 2 minutes.
